title: Implement MeshCore stream framing and adapter dispatcher
description: Why this exists: individual MeshCore operations need a common bounded framing, decode, dispatch, and response path. What needs to be done: implement reassembly, frame validation, output queueing, dispatch table, request correlation if required, and explicit unknown/unsupported handling. Acceptance criteria: tests cover valid frames, split frames, malformed length/checksum, oversized frames, unknown commands, disconnect cleanup, and output backpressure; dispatcher routes supported MVP operations to stub handlers; MeshCore RF packets are not passed to LICHEN LoRa as MeshCore RF.
notes:
Implementation evidence 2026-07-01: added board-agnostic MeshCore codec/adapter dispatcher with 0x3c/0x3e serial framing, command constants 0x01..0x41 from project-LICHEN-9c4y.13, deterministic ERR_UNSUPPORTED for unsupported/unknown commands, safe phase-1 read stubs, malformed length/range errors, serial reset cleanup, output backpressure accounting, and no RF/link calls. Gateway MeshCore BLE product path now starts a dispatcher pump from ble_meshcore_dequeue_rx() to ble_meshcore_enqueue_tx_if_session() guarded by session epochs; MeshCore RF packets are not passed to LICHEN LoRa.

Review round 1: Hegel the 2nd found P1 serial payload config could exceed stream_buf and P2 partial GET_CONTACTS response under backpressure. Zeno the 2nd found the same P1 plus P2 zero-length serial encoder mismatch, missing full command constants/coverage, and missing automated gateway adapter coverage. Fixes: BUILD_ASSERT serial payload <= frame max, nonzero serial encoder payload, full command enum 0x01..0x41, unsupported-command matrix test, tx_free preflight for multi-frame response, gateway native_sim fake-BLE adapter test.

Review round 2: Arendt the 2nd and Pauli the 2nd found remaining P2 that tx_free was optional for non-gateway callers. Fixed by requiring tx_free for multi-frame handlers, returning -ENOSYS before any CONTACTS_START enqueue when absent, documenting the contract in adapter.h, and adding test_multiframe_requires_capacity_callback.

Validation on EC2 instance i-05e27e074db786e22 in clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y4: git diff --check PASS. Direct native_sim: west build -p always -b native_sim lichen/tests/meshcore_adapter -d build-meshcore-adapter -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen && west build -d build-meshcore-adapter -t run PASS, meshcore_adapter 10/10. Direct native_sim: west build -p always -b native_sim lichen/tests/meshcore_gateway_adapter -d build-meshcore-gateway-adapter -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen && west build -d build-meshcore-gateway-adapter -t run PASS, meshcore_gateway_adapter 3/3. Twister: python3 /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN/zephyr/scripts/twister -T lichen/tests/meshcore_adapter -T lichen/tests/meshcore_gateway_adapter -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-meshcore-dispatch --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen PASS, 2/2 configurations, 13 test cases, 0 skipped, 0 warnings. Gateway product build: west build -p always -b native_sim lichen/apps/gateway -d build-gateway-meshcore-dispatch -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen -DEXTRA_CONF_FILE=/tmp/meshcore_gateway.conf PASS. Notable warnings: CONFIG_BT_FIXED_PASSKEY warning tracked by project-LICHEN-9c4y.17; no-sources warnings for loramac-node/drivers__net/drivers__spi are pre-existing Zephyr/native_sim warnings.
close_reason: Completed: MeshCore app dispatcher implemented and validated. Added bounded codec, serial framing, full command constants 0x01..0x41, deterministic unsupported/malformed handling, phase-1 stub responses, no-partial multi-frame backpressure behavior, gateway BLE dispatcher pump, and native_sim adapter/gateway tests. EC2 validation passed: direct meshcore_adapter 10/10, direct meshcore_gateway_adapter 3/3, Twister 2/2 configs with 13 cases and 0 warnings, MeshCore-enabled gateway native_sim build PASS. Reviews: initial P1/P2 findings fixed; final Arendt and Pauli follow-up reviews confirmed no P0/P1/P2 remaining.
